question: What is the thermal output of the NuScale Power Module (NPM)?

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling water, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, noting that some information is representative or conceptual and marked accordingly. The section also references figures illustrating the plant layout and system boundaries, and clarifies how security-related information is handled.

nuscale_ch01_0810.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.4 Detect and Suppress Method: Exclusion zone and buffer region methodology
nuscale_ch01_0810.summary: The NuScale design does not use the exclusion zone or buffer region methodology for thermal hydraulic stability, as reactor trip signals are designed to prevent conditions that could lead to flow instabilities before they develop. The existing reactor trip system (RTS) provides protection that is more limiting than required, and backup detect and suppress (D&S) options are not employed or needed. Overall, the RTS ensures compliance with safety criteria by preemptively tripping the reactor, rather than detecting and suppressing instabilities after they occur.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures that list and explain the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, system components, and safety systems, showing that NuScale uses a smaller, simpler, and more integrated design compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for easy reference.

nuscale_ch01_0070.title: Legends and Symbols
nuscale_ch01_0070.summary: This section provides legends and symbols used in NuScale’s Final Safety Analysis Report drawings, including detailed explanations for electrical symbols found in single-line diagrams. It describes the notation for various components such as transformers, circuit breakers, batteries, switches, and monitors, ensuring clarity and consistency in interpreting technical diagrams related to radioactive effluent flow paths and electrical systems.

nuscale_ch01_0811.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.5 Detect and Suppress Method Trip of reactor before SAFDL violation
nuscale_ch01_0811.summary: The NuScale design does not use the exclusion zone or detect-and-suppress methods for thermal hydraulic stability; instead, existing reactor trip signals act preemptively to prevent violation of safety limits before flow instabilities can develop. The system partially conforms to some regulatory criteria by providing more limiting protection than required, but does not employ specific detect-and-suppress options or related instrumentation, as these are deemed unnecessary for the design.

edges.description: Section A provides a general overview of the plant's systems and design, while Section B offers detailed drawings that visually clarify and supplement the descriptions found in Section A.
Section B provides specific nominal design values, such as the thermal output of the NuScale Power Module, which are referenced or summarized in the general descriptions found in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's systems and design, while Section B lists the legends, symbols, and figures used to interpret detailed system drawings referenced throughout the report.

Section A provides a general description of the plant design, which includes operating characteristics such as thermal output, while Section B discusses regulatory requirements for thermal hydraulic stability that the plant's operating characteristics (including thermal output) must satisfy.

Section B provides the nominal design parameters, including the thermal output, of the NuScale Power Module, which is the specific information needed to answer the analysis question, while Section A discusses thermal hydraulic stability methods and does not provide the thermal output value.

Section A describes the exclusion zone and buffer region methodology as not applicable to the NuScale design, while Section B specifies that reactor trip signals are used to prevent SAFDL violations, both addressing methods for detecting and suppressing thermal hydraulic instabilities in the context of regulatory review.
Section A references specific drawings and figures that are further detailed by the legends and symbols provided in Section B, enabling accurate interpretation of the diagrams.

Section A provides nominal design parameters, including thermal output, for the NuScale Power Module, while Section B discusses regulatory requirements for thermal hydraulic stability that the design must satisfy, thus connecting the design values in A to the regulatory criteria in B.
